source_jobs.title: Benefits Analyst (Contract, Remote)
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Nava is on a mission to #fixhealthcare. Nearly 160M Americans rely on their employers for healthcare — yet the system is broken, bloated, and dominated by incumbents who resist change. Nava fuses deep benefits expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver a modern, transparent, and affordable healthcare experience.

Founded by seasoned entrepreneurs and backed by leading investors, Nava is one of the fastest-growing benefits brokerages in the country. We’re the first to combine brokerage know-how with proprietary tech like HQ: our AI-powered benefits platform, designed to help HR teams take control of renewals, simplify strategy, eliminate spreadsheet chaos, and empower employees to navigate their benefits with ease.

In a $50B industry hungry for change, Nava is built to win — lowering costs for employers, delighting employees, and reshaping how healthcare works for millions of Americans.

About the Role

We’re hiring a Benefits Analyst Contractor to join our BA Pod and support with a variety of project work across our Client Success team. This is a contract role (40 hours/week) with the potential for extension. The role is remote and can be based anywhere in the U.S.

This position will focus on tactical, behind-the-scenes project work. No direct client ownership or client-facing meetings. It’s a great opportunity for someone who thrives on detail-oriented work and is comfortable with a contractor setup.

🚀 What you’ll do

In this role, you’ll provide surge capacity and project support to help our team deliver exceptional client service. Key responsibilities include:

 - Build and maintain renewal workbooks, including spreadsheeting, data validation, and rate modeling

 - Support benchmarking, plan design analysis, and other financial and strategic projects

 - Draft benefit communications and compliance-related documentation (e.g., ERISA Wraps, SPDs, SBCs)

 - Prepare open enrollment materials, employee communications, and benefit overviews

 - Support eligibility and benefits administration workflows across systems such as UKG, Employee Navigator, Gusto, Rippling, Paylocity, and ADP

 - Execute core eligibility tasks including processing enrollments and terminations, troubleshooting system errors, ordering ID cards, and managing OE changes

 - Assist with audits, census builds, data gathering, and maintaining accuracy across ben admin systems

 - Prepare snapshots, legal notices, and other recurring client deliverables

 - Conduct peer reviews and quality checks to ensure accuracy and consistency across deliverables

 - Support ad hoc benefits projects across the team as needed

🚀 Who will thrive in this role

 - You enjoy structured project work and thrive in roles that require precision and attention to detail

 - You’re comfortable jumping between project work and high-volume operational tasks, and can maintain accuracy in both

 - You’re comfortable operating independently while managing multiple priorities

 - You like being the go-to person behind the scenes who helps power great work

 - You’re familiar with brokerage operations and benefits deliverables

🚀 What you’ll bring

 - 3–10 years of experience in a benefits analyst, account manager or similar role within a benefits brokerage or consulting environment required

 - Familiarity with large group plans (100–5,000 MELs), California and New York market experience a plus

 - Strong Excel skills and the ability to work comfortably in Google Suite

 - Experience drafting benefits-related documents such as SPD/SBCs, ERISA Wraps, and enrollment materials in both languages

 - Understanding of financial structures such as fully insured, level-funded, self-funded, and captive plans

 - Professional fluency in Spanish and English (written and verbal) a plus

 - Detail-oriented mindset with a strong sense of ownership and follow-through

 - Availability to commit to 35-40 hour/week contract position

🚀 Contract Details

 - Duration: month to month, with po

source_jobs.title: Client Manager-Senior Client Manager - Large Group
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Nava is on a mission to #fixhealthcare. Nearly 160M Americans rely on their employers for healthcare — yet the system is broken, bloated, and dominated by incumbents who resist change. Nava fuses deep benefits expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver a modern, transparent, and affordable healthcare experience.

Founded by seasoned entrepreneurs and backed by leading investors, Nava is one of the fastest-growing benefits brokerages in the country. We’re the first to combine brokerage know-how with proprietary tech like HQ: our AI-powered benefits platform, designed to help HR teams take control of renewals, simplify strategy, eliminate spreadsheet chaos, and empower employees to navigate their benefits with ease.

In a $50B industry hungry for change, Nava is built to win — lowering costs for employers, delighting employees, and reshaping how healthcare works for millions of Americans.

About This Role & Why It Matters

We’re hiring a Large Group Client Manager-Senior Client Manager to join our East Coast Client Success team and support our New York client portfolio. This role is ideal for someone who can roll up their sleeves on the tactical work while also serving as a consultative benefits expert who leads client engagements with strategic insight. We’re specifically looking for experience independently managing large group employer relationships in the 100-2,000 life range. This is a fully remote role, with a strong preference for New York-based candidates or those with extensive large group experience who are willing to travel to New York for in-person client meetings.

What makes this opportunity unique: at Nava, you’ll be on the frontlines supporting our #fixhealthcare mission by helping employers and employees get more value from their benefits. You’ll have the chance to make an outsized impact, while working with industry leading AI-powered tools transforming how HR teams manage renewals and how employees experience their benefits. This is a chance to do the work you love, championing client relationships, guiding members through the benefits journey, and delivering creative strategies, while also helping us dismantle the outdated, antiquated practices that keep this industry stuck in the stone ages. At Nava, you get to be the change that makes benefits smarter, simpler, and actually work for people. Join us!

What You’ll Do

 - Own the Outcome of Your Book of Business: Manage a portfolio of New York large group employer clients (100–2000 lives), serving as their trusted advisor and proactively addressing needs throughout the plan year.

 - Strategic Planning and Execution: Lead annual service plan execution including renewals, open enrollment, compliance support, vendor deployment, and member education.

 - Innovate Benefits Strategies: Design creative, consultative benefit solutions based on strong knowledge of the New York market, alternative funding arrangements, carriers, and products.

 - Create and Present Client Deliverables: Develop and deliver materials such as renewal presentations, financial reporting, budget reviews, and education sessions for clients and members.

 - Enhance Client Experience: Collaborate with internal teams and vendor partners to streamline day-to-day servicing, ensure exceptional delivery, and strengthen Nava’s client success capabilities.

 - Manage and Deepen Relationships: Foster executive-level relationships with client leaders and create superfan clients by consistently delivering novel benefit solutions.

What You’ll Bring

We understand that your experience is more than just a list of requirements, so we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet all of the following bullet points.

 - Bachelor's Degree in a business-related field and/or equivalent education

 - 7-15 years of employee benefits experience with a brokerage

 - A minimum of 5+ years of recent experience independently managing accounts with minimal guidance or AE/Consultant support

 - Recent

source_jobs.title: Client Associate - Large Group
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Nava is on a mission to #fixhealthcare. Nearly 160M Americans rely on their employers for healthcare — yet the system is broken, bloated, and dominated by incumbents who resist change. Nava fuses deep benefits expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver a modern, transparent, and affordable healthcare experience.

Founded by seasoned entrepreneurs and backed by leading investors, Nava is one of the fastest-growing benefits brokerages in the country. We’re the first to combine brokerage know-how with proprietary tech like HQ: our AI-powered benefits platform, designed to help HR teams take control of renewals, simplify strategy, eliminate spreadsheet chaos, and empower employees to navigate their benefits with ease.

In a $50B industry hungry for change, Nava is built to win — lowering costs for employers, delighting employees, and reshaping how healthcare works for millions of Americans.


ABOUT THIS ROLE & WHY IT MATTERS

We’re hiring a Large Group Client Associate to join our Client Success team. This role is designed for a polished, detail-oriented benefits professional who is ready to step beyond pure analyst work and begin owning client relationships, while still partnering closely with Senior Client Managers and Account Executives on complex, evolving accounts.

This is an ideal next-step role for a Senior Benefits Analyst or junior Account Manager who is hungry for growth and wants the opportunity to both own a book of business and learn alongside seasoned consultants. You’ll act as a secondary Client Manager on some of our largest and most sophisticated employer groups (800–3,000 lives), while also independently managing a portfolio of smaller fully insured large groups (100–200 lives).

At Nava, this role sits at the intersection of technical excellence and relationship management. You’ll sharpen your consulting voice, expand your strategic exposure, and deepen your benefits expertise while directly contributing to our mission to #fixhealthcare. You’ll have access to modern, AI-powered tools that streamline the tactical work and give you more space to focus on what matters most: delivering meaningful outcomes for employers and employees.

This is a fully remote role, with a strong preference for California-based candidates or those with extensive large group experience who are willing to travel to California for in-person client meetings.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Support Complex Large Accounts as a Secondary CM: Partner with Account Executives on large, multi-layered employer groups (750–3,000 lives). Contribute to strategy, reporting, renewal preparation, vendor coordination, and client deliverables while learning advanced consulting approaches in real time.

 - Own a Book of Business Independently: Serve as the primary day-to-day contact for a portfolio of smaller large-group clients (100–200 lives). Manage plan-year servicing, respond to client inquiries, coordinate with carriers and vendors, and build strong, trust-based relationships.

 - Execute Core Benefits Analyst Responsibilities: Handle the tactical and analytical foundation of client servicing, including:
   
   - Spreading and analyzing financials for renewals
   
   - Carrier marketing and quoting support
   
   - Reporting and data analysis
   
   - Maintaining and configuring ben-admin systems
   
   - Eligibility and billing support
   
   - Vendor coordination and day-to-day servicing tasks

 - Lead Renewal & Open Enrollment Preparation: Build renewal and open enrollment presentations, develop employee education materials, support budget modeling, and ensure timelines are met across all stakeholders.

 - Create and Present Client Deliverables: Develop decks, reports, and financial summaries and confidently present to HR teams and client leadership for straightforward groups, while supporting senior teammates on more complex presentations.

 - Strengthen Client Experience: Collaborate cross-functionally with internal operations, member
